title: Bethany Base
wikitext:
[[Image:Eva2-22_C0041.jpg|right|thumb|Bethany Base Command Center and crew]] '''Bethany Base''' (ベタニアベース, ''Betania Bēsu'') is a branch of [[Nerv]] dedicated to the study and containment of the [[Third Angel]], located at the Old North Pole."At the old North Pole, and under control of the Euro branch." ''[[Evangelion 2.0: You Can (Not) Advance]]'' BD/DVD booklet. The base is a joint operation between [[Europe| European Union]] and [[Russia| Russian Federation]]''[[Evangelion 2.0: You Can (Not) Advance]]''. Subtitles by squall.leonhart, et al.
:'''Ritsuko''': With their Unit-05 lost, Europe and Russia are getting Asia involved with their claims., under the control of the EU's Third Branch, with operations announced in both English and Russian. The base itself is surrounded completely by water, and mostly contained within a submerged unit shaped like a giant chicken's egg (the smaller end pointing down).''[[Evangelion 2.0 Complete Records Collection]] The [[Command Center]] and its staff are housed within the Monitoring Command Ship ''[[Ural II]]'', on the ocean at a distance from the base. The base is designed to guard [[Evangelion Unit-05]]. In the place of an umbilical cable, the Eva receives power from tracks in the ceiling via extensions on the shoulder pylons. The walls of the facility are painted with numerous [[Seele]] logos and the logo is also displayed on the Third Angel's skull, suggesting significant Seele control over the base and the experiments conducted within. [[Mari Makinami Illustrious]] is stationed at the base at the beginning of ''[[Evangelion 2.0: You Can (Not) Advance|Eva 2.0]]'' with [[Ryoji Kaji]] being aboard the [[Ural II]] Command Ship. ==Areas and Meanings== {{Template:Relocate}} The base is divided into sections with names taken from the Greek underworld and Dante Alighieri's ''Inferno'', with the result that the 3rd Angel's attempted escape is likened to an escape from Hell itself. ===Limbo Area=== This appears to refer to the large egg-like structure constituting the majority of the base. The corridors of Styx (see below) are indicated by their official full designations to be within the Limbo Area, and, when the Angel slices through the ceiling of the Styx Perimeter Loop and escapes the "egg", a Bethany Base operator announces, "The target has broken through Limbo Area! Now moving into Acheron!" In ''Inferno'', Limbo is the first circle of Hell, lying just beyond the river Acheron. ===Cocytus=== The Angel containment facility at the bottom of the "egg", named for the the ninth and lowest circle of Hell in ''Inferno'', a frozen lake where Satan in detained. Since the 3rd Angel was discovered in permafrost and subsequently captured, the Cocytus facility, in keeping with its namesake, probably kept the Angel in deep freeze. The 3rd Angel's design, similar to a dragon or a snake, may accordingly be a nod to Satan. Cocytus is not seen in the film. Background dialogue prior to the Angel's first on-screen appearance states, "The 3rd Angel has breached the Final Cocytus System." ===Malebolge System=== Mentioned in background dialogue only. The system seems to constitute a series of obstacles intended to prevent the Angel from proceeding from Cocytus to the Styx Ring Shaft and then the Styx Main Shaft. It is activated in numbered pairs called Bolgia. In ''Inferno'', Malebolge is the eighth circle of Hell, a series of ten concentric circular ditches (each called a bolgia) surrounding Cocytus. ===Styx Shaft=== [[Image:Eva2-22 C0021 styx.jpg|thumb|Styx Shaft|left]] The Styx Shaft links the base from bottom to top and is outfitted for Eva-05's use. The Angel is said to enter the Styx Ring Shaft prior to moving into the Styx Main Shaft. In Greek mythology, the Styx is the river separating Earth from the Underworld, and it circles the latter nine times. In ''Inferno'', Styx is the fifth circle of Hell instead. ===Acheron=== [[Image:Eva2-22 C0040.jpg|right|thumb|Bethany Base rooftop area.]] Probably refers to the base's pillar-lined rooftop section. One of the operators announces that the Angel is "moving into Acheron" as it levitates through the outer hull, and the earlier line "We cannot allow [the Angel] to escape from Acheron!" further suggests that Acheron is the final safeguard measure. In ''Inferno'', the Acheron is the river bordering Hell, across which souls are ferried. ==Other Allusions== "Bethany" (Beth anya) is taken from the name of a Biblical village in the New Testament.[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bethany_%28biblical_village%29 Wikipedia. Bethany (biblical village).] Retrieved 2011-02-19. Incidentally, it was home to a Mary (''Mari''a, in Japanese) of Bethany, while Bethany Base is the location where we meet ''Mari''. ==Notes & References== {{reflist}} {{Org List}} [[Category:A to Z]] [[Category:Organizations]] [[Category:Places]] [[Category:Rebuild of Evangelion]] [[Category:Religious and Esoteric References]]
html:
Bethany Base (ベタニアベース, Betania Bēsu) is a branch of Nerv dedicated to the study and containment of the Third Angel, located at the Old North Pole.[1]

The base is a joint operation between European Union and Russian Federation[2], under the control of the EU's Third Branch[1], with operations announced in both English and Russian. The base itself is surrounded completely by water, and mostly contained within a submerged unit shaped like a giant chicken's egg (the smaller end pointing down).[3] The Command Center and its staff are housed within the Monitoring Command Ship Ural II,[3] on the ocean at a distance from the base.

The base is designed to guard Evangelion Unit-05. In the place of an umbilical cable, the Eva receives power from tracks in the ceiling via extensions on the shoulder pylons.

The walls of the facility are painted with numerous Seele logos and the logo is also displayed on the Third Angel's skull, suggesting significant Seele control over the base and the experiments conducted within.

Mari Makinami Illustrious is stationed at the base at the beginning of Eva 2.0 with Ryoji Kaji being aboard the Ural II Command Ship.

Contents

  • 1 Areas and Meanings
    • 1.1 Limbo Area
    • 1.2 Cocytus
    • 1.3 Malebolge System
    • 1.4 Styx Shaft
    • 1.5 Acheron
  • 2 Other Allusions
  • 3 Notes & References

Areas and Meanings[edit]

"Fortunately, the MAGI system can be transplanted. We can return to work tomorrow."
Some of the content in this article/section is better suited for inclusion in a different article (or possibly articles) and should be relocated.
Please discuss this issue on the talk page.

The base is divided into sections with names taken from the Greek underworld and Dante Alighieri's Inferno, with the result that the 3rd Angel's attempted escape is likened to an escape from Hell itself.

Limbo Area[edit]

This appears to refer to the large egg-like structure constituting the majority of the base. The corridors of Styx (see below) are indicated by their official full designations to be within the Limbo Area, and, when the Angel slices through the ceiling of the Styx Perimeter Loop and escapes the "egg", a Bethany Base operator announces, "The target has broken through Limbo Area! Now moving into Acheron!"

In Inferno, Limbo is the first circle of Hell, lying just beyond the river Acheron.

Cocytus[edit]

The Angel containment facility at the bottom of the "egg"[3], named for the the ninth and lowest circle of Hell in Inferno, a frozen lake where Satan in detained. Since the 3rd Angel was discovered in permafrost and subsequently captured, the Cocytus facility, in keeping with its namesake, probably kept the Angel in deep freeze. The 3rd Angel's design, similar to a dragon or a snake, may accordingly be a nod to Satan.

Cocytus is not seen in the film. Background dialogue prior to the Angel's first on-screen appearance states, "The 3rd Angel has breached the Final Cocytus System."

Malebolge System[edit]

Mentioned in background dialogue only. The system seems to constitute a series of obstacles intended to prevent the Angel from proceeding from Cocytus to the Styx Ring Shaft and then the Styx Main Shaft. It is activated in numbered pairs called Bolgia.

In Inferno, Malebolge is the eighth circle of Hell, a series of ten concentric circular ditches (each called a bolgia) surrounding Cocytus.

Styx Shaft[edit]

The Styx Shaft links the base from bottom to top and is outfitted for Eva-05's use. The Angel is said to enter the Styx Ring Shaft prior to moving into the Styx Main Shaft.

In Greek mythology, the Styx is the river separating Earth from the Underworld, and it circles the latter nine times. In Inferno, Styx is the fifth circle of Hell instead.

Acheron[edit]

Probably refers to the base's pillar-lined rooftop section. One of the operators announces that the Angel is "moving into Acheron" as it levitates through the outer hull, and the earlier line "We cannot allow [the Angel] to escape from Acheron!" further suggests that Acheron is the final safeguard measure.

In Inferno, the Acheron is the river bordering Hell, across which souls are ferried.

Other Allusions[edit]

"Bethany" (Beth anya) is taken from the name of a Biblical village in the New Testament.[4] Incidentally, it was home to a Mary (Maria, in Japanese) of Bethany, while Bethany Base is the location where we meet Mari.

Notes & References[edit]

  1. ↑ 1.0 1.1 "At the old North Pole, and under control of the Euro branch." Evangelion 2.0: You Can (Not) Advance BD/DVD booklet.
  2. ↑ Evangelion 2.0: You Can (Not) Advance. Subtitles by squall.leonhart, et al.
    Ritsuko: With their Unit-05 lost, Europe and Russia are getting Asia involved with their claims.
  3. ↑ 3.0 3.1 3.2 Evangelion 2.0 Complete Records Collection
  4. ↑ Wikipedia. Bethany (biblical village). Retrieved 2011-02-19.

Nerv Headquarters | Nerv 1st Branch | Nerv 2nd Branch | Nerv 3rd Branch
Nerv 7th Branch (Tabgha Base) | Bethany Base | Golgotha Base
